content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

July 18, 1991

Robert D. Beebe
General Manager
Dublin San Ramon Services District
7051 Dublin Boulevard
Dublin, CA 94568

Re: Your Request for Informal Assistance
Our File No. I-90-442

Dear Mr. Beebe:

     This letter is in response to your request for advice
regarding the duties and responsibilities of the Dublin San Ramon
Services District newly-appointed Administrative Services Manager/
Finance Officer under the disclosure provisions of the Political
Reform Act (the "Act").¹ Your request for advice does not include
a description of the duties of this new employee. Therefore, we
do not have all material facts pertinent to our analysis, so we
are providing you with informal assistance. (Regulation 18329.)²

QUESTION

     Under the provisions of the Act, what are the filing and
disclosure obligations of the Administrative Services Manager/
Finance Officer of the Dublin San Ramon Services District?

CONCLUSION

     Commission staff is now in the process of drafting
regulations to clarify the meaning of "public officials who manage
public investments" as that term is used in Section 87200, as

_______________________
   ¹ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission
regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section
18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

   ² Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the
immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice.
(Section 83114; Regulation 18329(c).)

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

Our File No. I-90-442
Page No. 2

amended.  Pending adoption of the regulations, the Administrative
Services Manager/Finance Officer of the Dublin San Ramon Services
District should continue to file Commission Form 730 Statement of
Economic Interests.

FACTS

The Dublin San Ramon Service District (the "district")
expects to appoint a new Administrative Services Manager/Finance
Officer.  Presumably, this individual will be involved in making
financial and investment decisions for the district.  You have
enclosed with your request for advice a copy of a proposed
investment program for the district.

You are the general manager for the district and in that
capacity you seek our advice to determine whether the
Administrative Services Manager/Finance Officer is required to
file Form 721 rather than Form 730 Statement of Economic Interests
upon assuming office.  Currently, the Administrative Services
Manager/Finance Officer files Commission Form 730.

ANALYSIS

Effective January 1, 1990, Section 87200 of the Government
Code was amended to add city treasurers, county treasurers, and
"other officials who manage public investments" to the list
of persons who must file Commission Form 721 Statement of Economic
Interests.  Any person who falls within one of these categories,
or is a candidate for any of these offices which is elective, is
now subject to the conflict-of-interest disclosure requirements of
the Act.

As I advised you during the course of our conversation on
June 4, 1991, staff is in the process of adopting regulations to
clarify the term "other public officials who manage public
investments" as that term is used in Section 87200.  Pending
adoption of the regulations, staff is providing interim advice
that persons who are "designated employees" in their agency's
conflict-of-interest code may continue to file a Form 730 State-
ment of Economic Interests with their filing officer.

Accordingly, the person discharging the duties of
Administrative Services Manager/Finance Officer for the district
should continue to file Commission Form 730 Statement of Economic
Interests.

As you are aware, your name has been previously added to the
list of interested persons who have requested information on the
subject.  We will continue to keep you informed of any progress in
the definition of the term "public officials who manage public
investments."

Our File No. I-90-442
Page No. 3


Should you have further questions regarding this matter, do
not hesitate to call me at (916) 322-5901.*

Sincerely,

Scott Hallabrin
Acting General Counsel

By: Bianca M. Breeze
Counsel, Legal Division

SH:BMB:dg


*
Copies of Commission regulations, opinions, and advice letters
may be obtained from the Commission at a cost of 10¢ per page.
